—

title: “4120 Project”

author: “Raymond Santiago”

date: “2026-05-03”

output: pdf_document

—

I. Background

National health and nutrition demographics help explore the factors influencing overall health.
Understanding the leading factors in Health and Nutrition is consequential for identifying the underlying health trends within the sample. This comes from the National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey. This can yield actionable insights to inform specialized community efforts to address concerns within communities at greatest risk.

investigating

II. Research Problem

With the waist-height ratio as the outcome variable of interest, the research questions were outlined. A series of research questions with the goals to explore current health standards across the participants of the study: Insert Observation source

  • What demographic, clinical, and lifestyle factors are significantly associated with the waist-to-height ratio in U.S. adults during the study period?

  • Among the risk factors, which ones best predict the waist-to-height ratio, for modifiable and non-modifiable risk factors?

Modifiable behaviors:

  • What behavior and lifestyle choices are associated with a higher WHR?

Non-Modifiable behaviors:

  • Which race and sex predictors are most significant, and which communities should be flagged for intervention?

Model 1: Stepwise
